doom.driver: Iran live updates: Trump says without ceasefire, 'one big glow' will come out of Iran. The U.S. conducted "self-defense" strikes against Iranian targets after Iran fired at U.S. destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, according to CENTCOM.
doom.blurb: TRUMP THREATENS IRAN WITH NUCLEAR GLOW
doom.summary: The President of the United States casually invoked what sounds very much like nuclear annihilation — 'one big glow' — as a negotiating tactic with Iran, while both sides are already exchanging live fire in the Strait of Hormuz despite a supposedly active ceasefire. Wall Street's traders have already coined the 'NACHO' trade betting Hormuz stays closed, which is the kind of thing that happens when financial professionals conclude the adults have definitively left the building. The ceasefire isn't ceasing, the fire is very much firing, and the rhetoric just escalated from 'deal or else' to something involving radioactive light.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Active military exchange in Hormuz, a shattered ceasefire, Trump's 'big glow' nuclear rhetoric, and North Korea deploying new long-range artillery targeting Seoul — multiple fronts lit up simultaneously.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: NATO allies are rethinking the alliance after Trump left them in the dark before striking Iran, while the EU faces a July 4 tariff ultimatum and the UK's Reform party surges in local elections.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Google's AlphaEvolve coding agent is scaling impact across scientific fields, and the 'agentic wars' between Meta and Google are heating up — capability is advancing faster than anyone is governing it.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A Dirtyfrag universal Linux local privilege escalation exploit dropped publicly, and ShinyHunters ransomware paralyzed thousands of US schools via the Canvas breach — cyber chaos is background noise now.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Anthropic published research on natural language autoencoders that peek inside Claude's reasoning — more interpretability work is quietly proceeding, which is one of the few genuinely reassuring signals today.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, night scene, no text, no antialiasing — vast darkness over the Strait of Hormuz at night, the narrow chokepoint water rendered in deep navy black, the hazy brown silhouette of the Iranian coastline a dim ridge on the northern horizon lit by distant orange smoldering glow — a single Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer sits dead center in the frame, its gray steel hull low on the ink-black water, the distinctive boxy SPY-1D radar array and forward 5-inch gun turret rendered in precise pixel detail, lit only by the ship's own running lights casting pale amber reflections on the water below — from the deck, twin white exhaust contrails of interceptor missiles curve upward into a sky thick with dark smoke, the trails fading into heavy shadow above — triangular delta-wing drone silhouettes hang motionless against the smoky sky, backlit by a massive deep orange fireball explosion blooming on the far right horizon where the Iranian coast meets the sea, the explosion casting the only warm light in the entire scene, painting the water surface in smoldering amber and copper — deep navy dominates everywhere, shadows swallow the water's edges, the smoke-filtered sky pressing down, warmth feels threatening — the reflected glow of the distant explosion shimmers broken across the destroyer's hull, the ship small against the enormous dark geography surrounding it.

dawn.driver: Child marriages plunged when girls stayed in school in Nigeria.
dawn.blurb: EDUCATION BREAKS THE CHAIN FOR NIGERIA'S GIRLS
dawn.summary: In a world of warring straits and missile exchanges, this finding cuts through the noise with quiet, durable hope: keeping girls in school directly collapsed child marriage rates in Nigeria. It's not a headline about a treaty or a summit — it's evidence that investing in education rewrites the future, one classroom at a time. Against a backdrop where pDoom sits at 39.1%, this is the kind of structural human progress that compounds across generations.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Rigorous research published in Nature links school enrollment directly to plummeting child marriage rates, giving policymakers a clear, evidence-based lever for change.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: The study reflects sustained collaboration between Nigerian institutions, international researchers, and education advocates working across borders.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Every girl who stayed in school and avoided early marriage represents an act of resilience against entrenched social pressure — multiplied across thousands of communities.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Educated girls grow into women who make more sustainable household and community resource decisions, with long-term ecological co-benefits.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: David Attenborough turns 100 today — a century of one human being dedicated to making the world care about life on Earth, still going strong.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Ground-level worm's-eye view looking straight up from the red laterite earth of a Northern Nigerian village school morning, the camera pressed low to the dirt road, gazing upward at a dramatic convergence of sky and life. The scene is dominated by towering acacia trees whose lacy canopies fracture deep golden amber dawn light into shards and speckles overhead, the sky behind them a radiant warm teal deepening toward the zenith. A modest concrete school building rises at the left edge, its corrugated iron roof catching the low side-lighting in sheets of burnished ochre, faded blue paint on its walls glowing in the early sun. Seen from below, a group of Nigerian schoolgirls aged twelve to seventeen walk directly overhead in silhouette and warm rim-lit detail — white blouses blazing in the amber backlight, deep blue skirts catching teal sky reflections — their worn notebooks and pencils clutched to their chests, the paper edges catching the light like little white flames. A younger sister walks among them, her small school bag strap visible against her shoulder, one hand wrapped around an older girl's fingers. The undersides of the girls' chins are lit gold by the rising sun. Long dramatic shadows stretch across the red earth toward the viewer. In the far background, receding and desaturated, a muted vignette of a traditional ceremony dissolves into shadow while the foreground blazes with forward momentum. Side-lighting with long shadows. Radiant.
